title: Saving for retirement
description: Practice understanding of starting saving , early, using 4% rule, calculating your contribution, and understanding increasing/decreasing costs in retirement.

assessment_items.question_content:
The table provides details about two individuals, Rhoda and Tay, who save for retirement. They both contribute to the [[☃ definition 1]].

**Description**          | **Rhoda** | **Tay** 
:-:|:-:|:-:
Monthly contribution     | $\$400$      | $\$200$      
Total years saving       | $10$         | $20$         
Total contributions      | $\green{\$48{,}000}$ | $\green{\$48{,}000}$ 

**Which person will have more money in their account at retirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They will both have equal amounts at retirement.

**Description**          | **Marty** | **Biff**

assessment_items.question_content:
The $4\%$ rule helps us decide how much to withdraw from our retirement savings each year to make sure our money lasts for about $30$ years.

Your 401(k) has a balance of $\$250{,}000$.

**How much can you safely withdraw from your 401(k)?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: The $4\%$ rule suggests that you can safely withdraw $4\%$ of your account value.
To find $4\%$ of the account value, we multiply the account value by $0.04$.
You can safely withdraw $\$$$\boxed{10{,}000}$ from your 401(k).

assessment_items.question_content:
The $4\%$ rule helps us decide how much to withdraw from our retirement savings each year to make sure our money lasts for about $30$ years.

Your 401(k) has a balance of $\$400{,}000$.

**How much can you safely withdraw from your 401(k)?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: The $4\%$ rule suggests that you can safely withdraw $4\%$ of your account value.
To find $4\%$ of the account value, we multiply the account value by $0.04$.
You can safely withdraw $\$$$\boxed{16{,}000}$ from your 401(k).

assessment_items.question_content:
The $4\%$ rule helps us decide how much to withdraw from our retirement savings each year to make sure our money lasts for about $30$ years.

Your IRA has a balance of $\$300{,}000$.

**How much can you safely withdraw from your IRA?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: The $4\%$ rule suggests that you can safely withdraw $4\%$ of your account value.
To find $4\%$ of the account value, we multiply the account value by $0.04$.
You can safely withdraw $\$$$\boxed{12{,}000}$ from your IRA.

assessment_items.question_content:
In retirement, many of your expenses may change. 

**Which of these is likely to decrease during retirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

As you think and plan for retirement, think of your expenses.

**Which of these expenses is likely to decrease in retirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Retirement brings changes to several spending categories.

**Which of these is likely to decrease during retirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In retirement, you may notice shifts in different expenses. 

**Which of these is likely to increase?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Retirement can bring changes to your finances.

**Which of these is likely to cost more when you retire?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

When you plan for retirement you have to consider how much things will cost once you retire.

**Which of these will likely become more expensive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
